free_text: Differential cross-section measurements for deuteron
elastic scattering on natSi for elastic
backscattering purposes
Natural Si target in form of 5*5 mm**2, 75 mm thick
Si3N4 foil with a ultra thin Au layer on the top
6 detectors positioned at angles 120, 130,
140, 150, 160, and 170 degrees
Elastic scattering of deuterons was
studied on natural Si in the energy range of 1.0-2.2
MeV. The differential cross sections on Si were
determined relative to the deuteron scattering cross
sections on Au, calculated using the Rutherford
formula. The deuteron energy at the half of the
target thickness was calculated using the SIMNRA
code. In order to validate the experimental data,
scattering spectra were also measured using a
polished crystalline Si[111] wafer as target.
systematical errors were estimated to
the statistical error on the experimental
yields
MPT
SD: Ref. updated, Author's name corrected.
